title: Key Management Operations from HSM and KMS Audit Trails

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 3.6.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 3.6.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.15: Key Management Operations from HSM and KMS Audit Trails.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.15' running on sourcetype aws:cloudtrail and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), custom HSM syslog parser
spl:
(index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventSource="kms.amazonaws.com" earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=hsm sourcetype="thales:luna:audit" earliest=-24h)
| eval op=coalesce(eventName, hsm_command, operation)
| where match(op,"(?i)delete|disable|import|unwrap|clone|partition")
| stats count by user, op, src_ip, host
| sort - count
description: Centralizes HSM partition and cloud KMS administrative events so cryptographic key custodians can prove dual-control and logging for keys that protect stored account data.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes aws, hsm and sourcetypes aws:cloudtrail, thales:luna:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Map IAM user/role to human custodian IDs. (2) Require ticket ID in CloudTrail session context where possible. (3) Alert on `ScheduleKeyDeletion` without CAB record. (4) Retain 12+ months in compliant storage tier. Indexes required: `index=aws`, `index=hsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail`, `sourcetype=thales:luna:audit`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.Intrusion_Detection by Intrusion_Detection.signature, Intrusion_Detection.dest, Intrusion_Detection.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), custom HSM syslog parser.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (`eventName` CreateKey, DisableKey, ScheduleKeyDeletion); `index=hsm` `sourcetype="thales:luna:audit"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map IAM user/role to human custodian IDs. (2) Require ticket ID in CloudTrail session context where possible. (3) Alert on `ScheduleKeyDeletion` without CAB record. (4) Retain 12+ months in compliant storage tier.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventSource="kms.amazonaws.com" earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=hsm sourcetype="thales:luna:audit" earliest=-24h)
| eval op=coalesce(eventName, hsm_command, operation)
| where match(op,"(?i)delete|disable|import|unwrap|clone|partition")
| stats count by user, op, src_ip, host
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Key Management Operations from HSM and KMS Audit Trails (PCI DSS Req 3.5.1, 3.6.1)** — Centralizes HSM partition and cloud KMS administrative events so cryptographic key custodians can prove dual-control and logging for keys that protect stored account data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (`eventName` CreateKey, DisableKey, ScheduleKeyDeletion); `index=hsm` `sourcetype="thales:luna:audit"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), custom HSM syslog parser. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws, hsm; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail, thales:luna:audit. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=aws, index=hsm, sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **op** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(op,"(?i)delete|disable|import|unwrap|clone|partition")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, op, src_ip, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (operations), table (custodian, op), pie (op mix).
grandmaExplanation: We centralizes HSM partition and cloud KMS administrative events so cryptographic key custodians can prove dual-control and logging for keys that protect stored account data. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Intrusion_Detection (CIM data model).
